behavior_summary: Implements a periodic NPC movement/idle process that wakes, checks distance to a context/origin and to a stored target reference, and either issues pathfinding toward the stored target or performs idle wandering and reschedules itself.

If the NPC is within a near threshold (<= 0x1E tiles) it prefers active behavior: if a stored target reference (local3) exists it will pathfind toward it; otherwise it runs npcIdleWander and requeues a follow-up check after a random delay (local1).
If the NPC is farther than the near threshold it schedules a fixed short retry delay (0x1E) instead of immediate pathfinding.

If a stored target reference (local3) is present but becomes too distant (> 0x40 tiles), the process resets internal reference slots and either triggers a general pathfind or a special one-off greeting + pathfind when the NPC identity and a story flag (arcadionMet == 0) match.
notable_control_flow: Periodic suspended loop: process suspends/resumes and performs checks each wake.
Near-vs-far branch based on FREE::getDistanceInTiles(this, 0x01) with threshold ~0x1E tiles decides idle vs reschedule behavior.
Target-validity branch: when local3 exists, compare FREE::getDistanceBetween(local3, this) to ~0x40 tiles to decide whether to reset references and change pathfinding behavior.
Special-case branch: if this == 0x39 and global flag arcadionMet == 0, call METHOD::func091C, play a bark 'Greetings Disciple.', then start a pathfind toward local3 (one-time greeting/pathfind).
Scheduling uses spawn->FREE::func1C19 with either a random delay (local1 from FREE::randomInRange) or a fixed delay (0x1E).
open_questions: Exact semantics and storage slots of local2/local3 (which reference slots they map to and whether they point to actors or locations) remain uncertain.
Precise purpose of METHOD::func091C called before the greeting pathfind (appears preparatory but details unknown).
Whether arcadionMet is the canonical flag name in game data or an inferred alias from context (inference based on naming).
The meaning of some low-confidence 'Unknown' nodes and how they affect edge-case timing or cleanup behavior.

main_function.name: U8P_MOVE::func0080
main_function.purpose: Drive an NPC's periodic movement/idle decision loop: choose between pathfinding toward a stored target, idle wandering, or rescheduling based on distance checks and reference validity; include a special-case one-time greeting+pathfind for a specific NPC identity.
entry_conditions: Runs as a suspended/resumable process (periodic loop).
Primary behavioral checks occur when the process wakes: distance to context/origin via FREE::getDistanceInTiles(this, 0x01) is evaluated (near threshold <= 0x1E).

function_map.name: U8P_MOVE::func0080
function_map.role: NPC movement/idle process (periodic loop controlling pathfinding, idle wandering, and re-scheduling).

external_calls.purpose: Measure tile-distance from this actor to a context/origin (used to decide near vs far behavior).
Measure distance between the stored target reference (local3) and this actor to detect stale/too-far targets (> 0x40) and trigger reference reset or alternate pathfinding.
Clear internal reference slots for this process when a stored target is invalid or too distant.
Start movement toward the stored target (local3); called with different pathfinding modes depending on branch (e.g., special-case vs general).
Make the NPC perform idle wandering when no active target is present and the actor is within the near threshold.
Schedule/reschedule a delayed invocation of behavior checks; used with random or fixed delays to control the process tick rate.
Generate the random delay value stored into local1 for scheduling idle-wander rechecks.
Preparatory call immediately before the special-case greeting and pathfind; likely sets animation/state/orientation but exact effect is unclear.

reads.name: arcadionMet
reads.likely_effect: Used as a story-state guard: when arcadionMet == 0 and this actor is id 0x39, allow a one-time greeting + pathfind. Appears to gate a scripted introductory interaction.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements a periodic NPC movement/idle process that wakes, checks distance to a context/origin and to a stored target reference, and either issues pathfinding toward the stored target or performs idle wandering and reschedules itself.
